items.title: Qwen3.6-27B: Flagship-Level Coding in a 27B Dense Model
items.author: mfiguiere
items.summary: Qwen3.6-27B is a 27B-parameter dense model that outperforms larger models in coding benchmarks. It offers flagship-level coding performance with lower deployment complexity. It's an open-weight model suitable for on-premises or privacy-sensitive coding assistants.

items.title: The Onion to Take over InfoWars
items.author: lxm
items.summary: The Onion has agreed to take over Infowars, aiming to mock conspiracy theories and repay debts to Sandy Hook families. The deal involves a monthly licensing fee for six months, with renewal options. The takeover follows a court-mandated auction won by The Onion in 2024.

items.title: OpenAI's response to the Axios developer tool compromise
items.author: shpat
items.summary: In March 2026, a compromised Axios developer tool exposed vulnerabilities in software supply chains. OpenAI confirmed no user data or intellectual property was accessed. They implemented new security measures.

items.title: Technical, cognitive, and intent debt
items.author: theorchid
items.summary: Technical debt refers to issues in the codebase, cognitive debt to team understanding erosion, and intent debt to missing externalized goals. These three types interact to affect software health, emphasizing the need for balanced management.

items.title: Parallel agents in Zed
items.author: ajeetdsouza
items.summary: Parallel agent execution in Zed is supported by opening multiple instances to bypass the single-agent bottleneck, significantly boosting productivity. This feature is actively discussed in GitHub issues and discussions.

items.title: Ars Technica: Our newsroom AI policy
items.author: zdw
items.summary: Ars Technica does not publish AI-generated content as authentic documentation but allows standard production edits. AI tools may assist with editing and workflow without displacing human authorship. Ars Technica's policy emphasizes maintaining the integrity of documentary media.

items.title: Bring your own Agent to MS Teams
items.author: umangsehgal93
items.summary: To bring your own agent to MS Teams, install Visual Studio Code, Node.js, and Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit. Create an Azure OpenAI service if needed, then use the toolkit to develop and deploy your agent in Teams.

items.title: Scoring Show HN submissions for AI design patterns
items.author: hubraumhugo
items.summary: A study found that 67% of Show HN submissions exhibit AI design patterns, with 21% showing heavy signs of AI-generated design. This indicates a significant trend in AI influence on web design.

items.title: Workspace Agents in ChatGPT
items.author: mfiguiere
items.summary: Workspace agents in ChatGPT automate complex tasks and workflows, leveraging the Codex model for persistent assistance across projects. They enhance productivity by handling multi-step processes without manual intervention. This feature is designed for businesses to streamline operations.

items.title: What killed the Florida orange?
items.author: danso
items.summary: The Florida orange industry was devastated by citrus greening disease and hurricanes. The industry lost over 95% of its production. The combination of disease and natural disasters killed the Florida orange.

items.title: The Neon King of New Orleans
items.author: renameme
items.summary: Jerry Therio is renowned for his collection of vintage neon signs. The band Kings of Neon is a New Orleans-based group known for their energetic performances. Therio has amassed over 800 neon pieces since 1978.

items.title: Windows 9x Subsystem for Linux
items.author: sohkamyung
items.summary: WSL9x is a project enabling Linux on Windows 9x systems, using a modified Linux kernel. It's not a Microsoft product but a community effort. It's not intended for serious use but for retrocomputing enthusiasts.

items.title: The Illuminated Man: an unconventional portrait of JG Ballard
items.author: agronaut
items.summary: The Illuminated Man is a biography of J. G. Ballard completed by Nina Allan after Christopher Priest's death. It explores Ballard's life and imagination. The book was published posthumously.

items.title: Tasteful Tokenmaxxing
items.author: omer_k
items.summary: Tokenmaxxing involves maximizing AI token usage to boost perceived productivity. It has sparked debate over AI spending efficiency. Some companies track and compete on token usage leaderboards.

items.title: An MCP Server for Fastmail – National Email Day
items.author: nmjenkins
items.summary: Fastmail launched an MCP server for its JMAP API on National Email Day. It allows interaction with email, calendar, and contacts. The server supports reading, searching, managing folders, and sending emails.

items.title: Columnar Storage Is Normalization
items.author: ibobev
items.summary: Columnar storage can sometimes replace normalization by organizing data by columns, which optimizes read performance but may reduce the need for joins, though normalization still has a role in data modeling.

items.title: OpenAI model for masking personally identifiable information (PII) in text
items.author: tanelpoder
items.summary: OpenAI offers a Privacy Filter model to detect and redact personally identifiable information in text. It can be customized to fit specific privacy needs. This tool helps ensure sensitive data is safely managed.

items.title: Our eighth generation TPUs: two chips for the agentic era
items.author: xnx
items.summary: Google introduced eighth-gen TPUs, TPU 8t and TPU 8i, to power the "Agentic Era" with nearly 3x higher compute performance. These chips are designed for training and inference, respectively.

items.title: Kernel code removals driven by LLM-created security reports
items.author: edward
items.summary: Kernel maintainers are removing networking code to address security bugs from large language models. Key removals include amateur radio and ATM protocols. This effort aims to reduce AI-generated bug reports.

items.title: Show HN: Broccoli, one shot coding agent on the cloud
items.author: yzhong94
items.text:
Hi HN — we built Broccoli, an open-source harness for taking coding tasks from Linear, running them in isolated cloud sandboxes, and opening PRs for a human to review.

We’re a small team, and our main company supplies voice data. But we kept running into the same problem with coding agents. We’d have a feature request, a refactor, a bug, and some internal tooling work all happening at once, and managing that through local agent sessions meant a lot of context switching, worktree juggling, and
items.summary: Broccoli is an open-source coding agent running in isolated cloud sandboxes to automate coding tasks and open pull requests for review. It aims to reduce context switching and manage multiple coding tasks efficiently. It's an alternative to existing cloud coding agents.

items.title: 3.4M Solar Panels
items.author: marklit
items.summary: 3.4 million solar panels have been deployed, with an additional 500,000 added recently. This expansion includes new data sources. The current date is April 23, 2026.
